title: trigonella polyceratia subsp. amandiana
intro: Trigonella polyceratia subsp. amandiana, also known as Ammi Visnaga, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and also has been used to treat various conditions including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
